title: Abarema maestrensis
taxonomy: Abarema maestrensis is classified within the plant family Fabaceae, also known as legumes or pea family. It belongs to the subfamily Caesalpinioideae and the genus Abarema. The genus Abarema is a group of tropical trees native to the Americas and the Caribbean, and Abarema maestrensis is one of the species within this genus. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant. Abarema maestrensis is a distinct species within this classification.

intro: Abarema maestrensis, also known as the Cuban jicaro or Mayan jicaro, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the Caribbean region. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Abarema maestrensis include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
